workflow_runs.name: DevX Tag
workflow_runs.display_title: chore: move tool_call_parser resolution from config validation to ser…

head_commit.message:
chore: move tool_call_parser resolution from config validation to server startup (#1882)

The auto-detection ran at config time via a model_validator, but the model
name may not be final yet (e.g. RLConfig propagates it later), requiring a
hacky reset+re-resolve in auto_setup_model(). Moving the lookup to
server() ensures we always resolve against the final model name.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

workflow_runs.name: Release
workflow_runs.display_title: fix device mesh flattened dim deprecation warning (#1874)

head_commit.message:
fix device mesh flattened dim deprecation warning (#1874)

* Fix DeviceMesh flattened dim deprecation warning (PT 2.10+)

Store _flatten() return values in a _submeshes dict and access them
via get_mesh() instead of going through world_mesh.__getitem__, which
triggers "Slicing a flattened dim from root mesh will be deprecated
in PT 2.11" warnings and will break in PT 2.11.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix get_mesh lazy init ordering

Ensure world_mesh (and _submeshes) are initialized before checking
the submeshes dict, so synthetic names like "hsdp" don't fall through
to the root mesh lookup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

workflow_runs.name: CPU Tests
workflow_runs.display_title: fix device mesh flattened dim deprecation warning (#1874)

head_commit.message:
fix device mesh flattened dim deprecation warning (#1874)

* Fix DeviceMesh flattened dim deprecation warning (PT 2.10+)

Store _flatten() return values in a _submeshes dict and access them
via get_mesh() instead of going through world_mesh.__getitem__, which
triggers "Slicing a flattened dim from root mesh will be deprecated
in PT 2.11" warnings and will break in PT 2.11.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix get_mesh lazy init ordering

Ensure world_mesh (and _submeshes) are initialized before checking
the submeshes dict, so synthetic names like "hsdp" don't fall through
to the root mesh lookup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: wandb logging order (#1880)

* fix: pass explicit step to wandb.log in orchestrator

Without an explicit step, wandb auto-increments its internal counter,
causing subsequent calls with explicit step values to trigger
"step N is less than current step N+1" warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use explicit None check for step=0 in eval_utils

`step or ckpt_step` treats step=0 as falsy, silently substituting
ckpt_step. Use `step if step is not None else ckpt_step` instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* simplify: step is always an int, drop Optional and fallback

Both call sites always pass step=progress.step (int), so remove the
None type and the ckpt_step fallback that was broken for step=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: commit immediately on wandb.log to avoid delayed dashboard data

With explicit step=, wandb accumulates without committing until it sees
a new step. Adding commit=True flushes data to the dashboard immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "fix: commit immediately on wandb.log to avoid delayed dashboard data"

This reverts commit bf679db5f4069374a74d409c0064cd04d8091c6b.

* fix: add monitor.flush() for immediate wandb commits

commit=True can't be used on individual wandb.log calls when multiple
calls share the same step (it advances the counter, breaking subsequent
calls). Instead, accumulate with the default commit=False, then flush
once at the end of the step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

workflow_runs.name: Release
workflow_runs.display_title: chore: consolidate config modules into prime_rl.configs (#1871)

head_commit.message:
chore: consolidate config modules into prime_rl.configs (#1871)

* consolidate config modules into prime_rl.configs subpackage

Move all scattered config.py files (utils, trainer, rl, sft, orchestrator,
env_server, inference, transport, rl_config) into a single prime_rl.configs
subpackage for better discoverability and organization. Update all imports
across 59 files and refresh skill docs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* merge shared, transport, and utility configs into configs/shared.py

Move SharedLog/Wandb/Checkpoint/Model/WeightBroadcast configs, deployment
configs, transport configs, and common utility configs (ModelConfig,
ClientConfig, LogConfig, WandbConfig, etc.) into a single configs/shared.py.
Remove configs/transport.py and configs/utils.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* rename SFTTrainerConfig -> SFTConfig, sft_trainer.py -> sft.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* move shared configs into rl configs

* move trainer configs into shared.py

Merge configs/trainer.py into configs/shared.py since these configs
(model, optimizer, scheduler, checkpoint, LoRA, SLURM, etc.) are shared
across both SFT and RL trainers. Rename ModelConfig -> TrainerModelConfig
to avoid collision with the common ModelConfig.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* move some stuff

* update configs

* clean up config naming: move trainer configs, drop component prefixes

- Rename rl_trainer.py -> trainer.py, RLTrainerConfig -> TrainerConfig
- Move shared trainer building blocks (ModelConfig, optimizers, schedulers,
  checkpointing, LoRA, etc.) from shared.py into trainer.py
- Rename TrainerModelConfig -> ModelConfig, common ModelConfig -> BaseModelConfig
- Rename TrainerCheckpointConfig -> CheckpointConfig
- Drop Orchestrator/Inference prefixes from config classes
  (OrchestratorModelConfig -> ModelConfig, InferenceModelConfig -> ModelConfig, etc.)
  since the module name provides context; use aliases in rl.py for disambiguation
- Fix OrchestratorModelConfig inheriting from training ModelConfig instead of BaseModelConfig
- Fix broadcast module imports that had mismatched class names

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* update changelog with config consolidation entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

(model, optimizer, scheduler, checkpoint, LoRA, SLURM, etc.) are shared
across both SFT and RL trainers. Rename ModelConfig -> TrainerModelConfig
to avoid collision with the common ModelConfig.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* move some stuff

* update configs

* clean up config naming: move trainer configs, drop component prefixes

- Rename rl_trainer.py -> trainer.py, RLTrainerConfig -> TrainerConfig
- Move shared trainer building blocks (ModelConfig, optimizers, schedulers,
  checkpointing, LoRA, etc.) from shared.py into trainer.py
- Rename TrainerModelConfig -> ModelConfig, common ModelConfig -> BaseModelConfig
- Rename TrainerCheckpointConfig -> CheckpointConfig
- Drop Orchestrator/Inference prefixes from config classes
  (OrchestratorModelConfig -> ModelConfig, InferenceModelConfig -> ModelConfig, etc.)
  since the module name provides context; use aliases in rl.py for disambiguation
- Fix OrchestratorModelConfig inheriting from training ModelConfig instead of BaseModelConfig
- Fix broadcast module imports that had mismatched class names

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* update changelog with config consolidation entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

workflow_runs.name: CPU Tests
workflow_runs.display_title: cleaner naming for discriminated unions in config (#1879)

head_commit.message:
cleaner naming for discriminated unions in config (#1879)

* Drop Type suffix from config union TypeAliases

Rename all *ConfigType aliases to drop the redundant suffix:
- OptimizerConfigType → OptimizerConfig
- SchedulerConfigType → SchedulerConfig
- DeploymentConfigType → DeploymentConfig
- SFTDeploymentConfigType → SFTDeploymentConfig
- DataConfigType → DataConfig
- TransportConfigType → TransportConfig
- FilterConfigType → FilterConfig
- WeightBroadcastConfigType → WeightBroadcastConfig
- LossConfigType → LossConfig (class renamed to DefaultLossConfig)
- AdvantageConfigType → AdvantageConfig (class renamed to DefaultAdvantageConfig)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move Field(discriminator) into TypeAliases, simplify field sites

Embed Field(discriminator="type") in each union TypeAlias so field
declarations can use the alias directly without wrapping in
Annotated[..., Field(discriminator="type")].

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
